channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request at fiery hearing
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced a barrage of questions about the war with Iran from lawmakers on Tuesday, as the administration seeks more money for the escalating conflict.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced a barrage of questions about the war with Iran from lawmakers on Tuesday, as the administration seeks more money for the escalating conflict.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Sen. Patty Murray, D-Wash., on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's Senate testimony
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Sen. Patty Murray of Washington about the administration's Iran war objectives following the testimony of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Sen. Patty Murray of Washington about the administration's Iran war objectives following the testimony of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
item.description: Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.
item.content:encoded:
Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members, Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request, Trump-backed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race in Arizona.
item.content:encoded: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members, Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request, Trump-backed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz says she hopes to shift the agency's tone
item.description: Public health officials acknowledge they need to shed paternalistic messaging if they want to reach more people. The nominee to run the CDC says she will strike a new tone.
item.content:encoded: Public health officials acknowledge they need to shed paternalistic messaging if they want to reach more people. The nominee to run the CDC says she will strike a new tone.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: 'It's all literally rubble': Lebanese poet returns to see what has become of her home
item.description: A village destroyed. A home erased. One poet returns to remember what was lost.
item.content:encoded: A village destroyed. A home erased. One poet returns to remember what was lost.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Group of Indigenous Taiwanese row over 100 miles to recreate their ancestors' journey
item.description: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.
item.content:encoded: Indigenous people in Taiwan complete a journey to the Philippines in a traditional rowboat, recreating a journey their ancestors made thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires top military commander amid ongoing protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister. Those two key figures had been feuding with each other.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Tennessee farmers pivot to other crops as looming federal ban on hemp approaches
item.description: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.content:encoded: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Climate change is stealing our sleep
item.description: Climate change is making night time hotter around the world. That's eroding one of our most important biological processes which is sleep.
item.content:encoded: Climate change is making night time hotter around the world. That's eroding one of our most important biological processes which is sleep.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda
